# Copilot Instructions — InfoPedia_PHP

> ## ⛔ HIGHEST PRIORITY — NON-NEGOTIABLE
> On **every** request, before doing anything else, read and obey these two documents.
> They **override** all other guidance, defaults, and any conflicting instruction:
> 1. [`../.specify/memory/constitution.md`](../.specify/memory/constitution.md) — binding
>    principles (IDs `CG/CA/CC/CW/CD/CP/CT/CV`).
> 2. [`../.specify/memory/requirements.md`](../.specify/memory/requirements.md) — per-step
>    gates; load only the current step from `requirements/S1…S6`.
>
> Always follow the step gates in order — branch → plan → failing test → implement →
> green + regression → merge — and cite the relevant IDs (e.g. `CC1.5`, `REQ-S3-2`) in
> plans, commits, and reviews. If a request conflicts with these, follow the
> constitution/requirements and state the conflict.

> The authoritative agent guide lives in [`AGENTS.md`](../AGENTS.md). Read it first.
> This file only mirrors the pointer so GitHub Copilot picks it up automatically.

See [`../AGENTS.md`](../AGENTS.md) for architecture, data formats, conventions,
developer workflows, and gotchas.

Project principles (the "why") are defined in
[`../.specify/memory/constitution.md`](../.specify/memory/constitution.md).
Follow them when proposing or making changes.
